title: National Accreditation Scheme for Salmonella Testing Laboratories
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A   National   Accreditation     Scheme     for     Salmonella   Testing   Laboratories     is     essential     to     strengthen     food     safety     systems     and     reduce     the     burden     of     foodborne     illness  .   Accreditation     ensures     that     laboratories     meet     internationally     recognized     standards     for     quality  ,   competence  ,   and     reliability   in   Salmonella     detection  .   This     policy     will     help     harmonize   testing   methods  ,   minimize     false     positives     or     negatives  ,   and     improve     the     traceability     of     results     across     regions  . By   implementing     a     standardized     accreditation     framework  ,   governments     can     increase     confidence   in   laboratory   data,   support     timely     public     health     interventions  ,   and     enhance     compliance     with     domestic     and     international     food     safety     regulations  . The   scheme     will     also     facilitate     trade     by     aligning     with   global   benchmarks  ,   fostering     consumer     trust  ,   and     ensuring     that     food     products     entering     markets   are   verified     safe     and     free     from     Salmonella     contamination  .
